title: Lower Face Botox Injection Depth Masterclass | Avoid Common Mistakes & Optimize Results

excerpt: In this video, I'm going to give you a master class on depth of injection for botulinam toxins. This is where most injectors actually go wrong, and it's actually not the thing that's taught in most day courses. We focus on where to inject, but not how deep.

segments.text: In this video, I'm going to give you a master class on depth of injection for botulinam toxins. This is where most injectors actually go wrong, and it's actually not the thing that's taught in most day courses. We focus on where to inject, but not how deep. In every area of the face, you will get subtly different results depending on the depth. By understanding depth, you'll be empowered to inject more accurately, get more predictable results, and fewer side effects. There's a key stage in your path to success where you suddenly stop thinking about injection points and you start to know how risk and reward changes with every shift in angle, depth, and position of your needle. And that's when you suddenly become free to just create the results you want to create because you are automatically guided. Let me share with you what goes through my mind at different injection depths while I'm injecting botuly toxins and how it guides me to optimize my treatment results. the pressure. Intrammal injections with toxin will sting. Go a little bit deeper, they hurt a little bit less. And that's usually where I try and inject on the surface of the muscle where the receptors are likely to be or potentially slightly deeper. But if you keep injecting deeper still, you're going to squirt bottom toxin into a patient's mouth and that's a waste of money. The next area is the depressor angularis orus. This muscle pulls the corner of your mouth down. It's also layered in a particular way with the depressor labi muscle. If you don't know which is which, you'll soon find out when your patient comes back unhappy when you've hit the depressor labi muscle and not hit the depressor angularis orus. So we need to first establish the position of injecting which I do by feeling for the lateral aspect of the contracted depressor angularis orus muscle here. But the next thing is to know about the depth. Have a look at this model and you can see how the depth relates to the depressor labiari muscle. The most superficial muscle is the depressor angularis orus and this is typically where I like to

inject. However, if you go deep to it, particularly if you're inferior, you may go straight through and hit this muscle instead, the depressor labi muscle. Depressor labi pulls your mouth open when you smile. Depressor angularis orus only pulls the corner of your mouth down. So what you get if you go too deep and too medial is that you no longer smile properly in the medial part of the lip. Some people call this the pey smile because one side of the mouth moves and the other one doesn't and it causes a very obvious asymmetry. In terms of depth we go through the dermis through the hypodermis and then stop not going all the way through to the level of the perryioium. Of course you need to carefully assess this depending on the size of the superficial fat pad above this muscle. Next time you're injecting pay attention to the level that you're at. Start to think with higher resolutions. And this is key to becoming a much more proficient and confident injector by understanding what is going on, where your needle tip is likely to be residing, and therefore how you can optimize it with different depths, angles or techniques or doses. one of the core elements of becoming really confident with botulym toxins.
